title: Python: Beginner to Intermediate
summary: A Python 3.14 course from first expressions through collections, functions, modules, files, exceptions, classes, iteration, typing, packaging, testing and asynchronous basics.

assumed_knowledge: Programming Foundations or equivalent diagnostic mastery
audience.target_capability: Independently design, package, test and debug a multi-module Python program using idiomatic data structures, explicit interfaces and appropriate standard-library tools.

includes: Files, JSON, context managers and exceptions
Classes, data classes and object protocols
Type hints, tests, logging and debugging

runtime.execution_environment: Isolated Linux container with deterministic dependency, memory, CPU and time limits
notes: The official tutorial assumes prior programming, so absolute-beginner mental models remain in Programming Foundations.
Third-party frameworks and data libraries are separately versioned extensions.

modules.title: Interpreter, scripts and diagnostics
modules.description: Run experiments and saved programs while understanding Python's execution environment.

skills.title: Interactive interpreter
skills.description: Use the REPL for bounded exploration and distinguish expression results from printed output.
outcomes: Test an expression in the REPL and transfer the verified behavior into a script.

skills.title: Scripts, indentation and main guard
skills.description: Create executable modules with meaningful layout, indentation and optional entry-point guard.
outcomes: Write and run a saved script while explaining its top-level execution.

skills.title: Interpreter and environment
skills.description: Identify interpreter version, arguments, working directory, module path and environment effects.
outcomes: Diagnose a script that runs differently because of interpreter or working-directory selection.

skills.title: Tracebacks and error categories
skills.description: Read syntax diagnostics and exception tracebacks from the relevant frame and causal chain.
outcomes: Classify and repair representative syntax, runtime and logic failures.

modules.title: Values, expressions and control flow
modules.description: Use Python's value model and structured control flow deliberately.

skills.title: Numbers, booleans, None and text
skills.description: Use core immutable scalar values and distinguish type, value and truthiness.
outcomes: Choose and operate on suitable scalar values while explaining precision and absence semantics.

skills.title: Operators and comparisons
skills.description: Evaluate arithmetic, Boolean, membership, identity and chained comparisons correctly.
outcomes: Trace expressions and repair identity-versus-equality and precedence defects.

skills.title: if and match
skills.description: Use conditions and structural pattern matching for complete and readable decisions.
outcomes: Implement and test boundary-complete selection using an appropriate construct.

skills.title: for, while and loop control
skills.description: Iterate over values directly and use condition-controlled loops with explicit progress.
outcomes: Implement and debug collection, counted and sentinel loops without needless index manipulation.

modules.title: Core collections and text
modules.description: Select, mutate and traverse built-in data structures with aliasing awareness.

skills.title: Lists, tuples and slicing
skills.description: Use mutable sequences, immutable records and bounded slicing operations.
outcomes: Implement sequence transformations while distinguishing mutation, copying and rebinding.

skills.title: Dictionaries and sets
skills.description: Use key-value lookup and unique membership with hashable keys.
outcomes: Choose and use a dictionary or set for counting, indexing and deduplication problems.

skills.title: Mutability, aliases and copying
skills.description: Distinguish shared references, shallow copies and independent nested values.
outcomes: Trace and repair accidental shared mutation in nested collections.

skills.title: Unicode text and formatting
skills.description: Use string methods, f-strings, splitting and joining while treating text as Unicode.
outcomes: Implement and test robust text normalization, parsing and formatting.

modules.title: Functions, scope and recursion
modules.description: Design clear callable interfaces and understand Python argument binding.

skills.title: Function contracts and returns
skills.description: Write cohesive functions with documented inputs, outputs, effects and failure behavior.
outcomes: Design, implement and test a function with a clear docstring contract.

skills.title: Parameter kinds and argument binding
skills.description: Use positional, keyword, default, variadic and keyword-only parameters without mutable-default traps.
outcomes: Trace argument binding and repair an unsafe or ambiguous function signature.

skills.title: Scope and closures
skills.description: Resolve local, enclosing, global and built-in names and capture enclosing state deliberately.
outcomes: Trace name lookup and implement a small closure without accidental shared mutation.

skills.title: Recursive functions
skills.description: Implement terminating recursion and compare it with iterative alternatives under Python's call limits.
outcomes: Write, trace and test a recursive routine with explicit base and progress cases.

modules.title: Modules, packages and environments
modules.description: Organize reusable code and reproduce its dependency environment.

skills.title: Modules and imports
skills.description: Define module APIs and reason about import execution, names and search paths.
outcomes: Split a script into modules and diagnose a name or import-path failure.

skills.title: Packages and public APIs
skills.description: Organize related modules, use relative imports sparingly and expose a deliberate package interface.
outcomes: Create and import a small package with clear internal and public names.

skills.title: Virtual environments and dependencies
skills.description: Create isolated environments and install only declared dependencies.
outcomes: Reproduce a project environment from documented interpreter and dependency requirements.

skills.title: Project metadata and command entry
skills.description: Use pyproject metadata and a conventional source/test layout for a small application.
outcomes: Package and invoke a multi-module command-line application reproducibly.

modules.title: Exceptions, files and resources
modules.description: Handle expected failures and persist structured data through safe boundaries.

skills.title: Exception handling and propagation
skills.description: Catch specific exceptions at responsible boundaries and preserve causal context.
outcomes: Trace exception propagation and implement a narrow recovery policy.

skills.title: Raising and custom exceptions
skills.description: Validate contracts and define domain exceptions only when callers benefit from the distinction.
outcomes: Design and test failure behavior for a small module API.

skills.title: Files, paths and JSON
skills.description: Read and write text with explicit encoding and validate structured JSON data.
outcomes: Implement file persistence that handles missing, malformed and permission-failure cases.

skills.title: Context managers
skills.description: Use with-statements to guarantee resource cleanup and understand the enter/exit protocol conceptually.
outcomes: Refactor manual file cleanup into a failure-safe context-managed workflow.

modules.title: Classes and object protocols
modules.description: Model valid objects and cooperate with Python's standard protocols.

skills.title: Classes, instances and attributes
skills.description: Define instance state and methods while distinguishing class and instance attributes.
outcomes: Design and implement a class with clear invariants and no accidental shared mutable class state.

skills.title: Data classes and value modeling
skills.description: Use data classes for transparent values and custom classes when invariants or behavior require them.
outcomes: Choose and implement an appropriate data class or regular class for a domain model.

skills.title: Composition, inheritance and super
skills.description: Prefer composition and use inheritance only for substitutable behavior with cooperative initialization.
outcomes: Implement or refactor a small design using justified composition or inheritance.

skills.title: Representation, equality and container protocols
skills.description: Implement selected special methods to make domain values predictable in display, comparison and iteration.
outcomes: Implement and test appropriate repr, equality or container protocol behavior without surprising side effects.

modules.title: Comprehensions, iterators and decorators
modules.description: Use Python's iteration abstractions for readable lazy and eager transformations.

skills.title: Comprehensions
skills.description: Use list, set and dictionary comprehensions for bounded, readable transformations.
outcomes: Choose between a comprehension and explicit loop and justify readability and memory implications.

skills.title: Iterable and iterator protocols
skills.description: Distinguish reusable iterables from stateful one-pass iterators.
outcomes: Trace iteration state and implement a small iterable or iterator correctly.

skills.title: Generators and lazy pipelines
skills.description: Use yield and generator expressions for lazy stateful production with finite-consumption awareness.
outcomes: Implement and test a lazy generator pipeline without accidental exhaustion.

skills.title: Decorator basics
skills.description: Wrap callables while preserving metadata and making behavior changes explicit.
outcomes: Implement and explain a small function decorator with correct argument forwarding.

modules.title: Typing, tests, logging and debugging
modules.description: Make interfaces understandable and verify behavior with repeatable evidence.

skills.title: Type hints and static checking concepts
skills.description: Annotate public interfaces using unions, collections, protocols and generics without treating hints as runtime enforcement.
outcomes: Annotate and review a module API and explain what remains a runtime responsibility.

skills.title: Automated tests
skills.description: Use isolated fixtures, assertions, subtests and mocks only at genuine external boundaries.
outcomes: Create a deterministic unit and integration test suite covering normal, boundary and failure behavior.

skills.title: Logging and diagnostics
skills.description: Emit structured severity-appropriate diagnostics without leaking secrets or replacing exception handling.
outcomes: Add useful operational logging and distinguish it from user-facing output.

skills.title: Debugger and final project
skills.description: Use breakpoints, state inspection and tests to complete a packaged command-line application.
outcomes: Deliver and explain a typed, tested, logged multi-module CLI project with reproducible setup.

modules.title: Asynchronous and concurrent work basics
modules.description: Choose a concurrency model based on waiting, CPU work and shared-state constraints.

skills.title: Concurrency versus parallelism
skills.description: Distinguish interleaved progress, simultaneous execution, I/O waiting and CPU-bound work.
outcomes: Classify a workload and explain whether concurrency can improve responsiveness or throughput.

skills.title: asyncio coroutines and tasks
skills.description: Use async, await and task coordination for bounded I/O-style examples without blocking the event loop.
outcomes: Implement and trace a small asynchronous workflow with timeout and cancellation handling.

skills.title: Threads and processes
skills.description: Choose threads for compatible waiting work and processes for isolated CPU work while accounting for communication cost.
outcomes: Select and justify sequential, asynchronous, threaded or process-based execution for a stated task.

skills.title: Shared-state and cancellation safety
skills.description: Recognize races, protect invariants and guarantee cleanup across failure or cancellation.
outcomes: Repair a teaching example with unsafe shared state or lost cleanup.

exit_outcomes: Build and diagnose a reproducible, packaged, multi-module Python 3.14 command-line application.
Use Python's data model, built-in collections, functions, objects and iteration protocols idiomatically.
Handle files, structured data, exceptions, environments and dependencies through explicit boundaries.
Demonstrate behavior with type-informed interfaces, automated tests, logging and debugger evidence.
Choose an appropriate sequential or basic concurrent execution model for a stated workload.
